evals.prompt: We've been building a vector similarity search feature using FAISS. I keep seeing references to HNSW as the index type but I don't fully understand what it is or why it's preferred. tutorialize HNSW
evals.expected_output: A Markdown tutorial file saved to the Obsidian vault at HNSW.md, covering what HNSW is, why it's used for approximate nearest neighbor search, how it compares to flat indexes, and graph traversal intuition.

assertions.name: vault_file_created
assertions.description: A .md file was written to the Obsidian vault at /Users/bianders/morphy/ (with_skill only)

assertions.name: filename_title_case
assertions.description: Filename is 'HNSW.md' — correct casing, no hyphens or lowercasing

assertions.name: context_anchored
assertions.description: Content references FAISS or vector similarity search — shows the skill used conversation context, not a generic explanation

evals.prompt: I'm fine-tuning a sentence embedding model and the loss function in the training config is set to 'contrastive'. I don't know what contrastive loss is — tutorialize it
evals.expected_output: A Markdown tutorial file saved to the Obsidian vault at Contrastive Loss.md, covering what contrastive loss is, how positive/negative pairs work, why it's used for embedding training, and intuition for the margin parameter.

assertions.name: vault_file_created
assertions.description: A .md file was written to the Obsidian vault at /Users/bianders/morphy/ (with_skill only)

assertions.name: filename_title_case
assertions.description: Filename is 'Contrastive Loss.md' — title case with space, no hyphens

assertions.name: context_anchored
assertions.description: Content references sentence embeddings, fine-tuning, or the specific use case — not a generic loss function overview

evals.prompt: I'm building a FastAPI endpoint and using Pydantic for request validation. I've seen @validator and @field_validator decorators in example code but don't understand when to use them or how they work. tutorialize Pydantic validators
evals.expected_output: A Markdown tutorial file saved to the Obsidian vault at Pydantic Validators.md, covering what Pydantic validators are, the difference between field_validator and model_validator, pre vs post validation, and practical FastAPI usage examples.

assertions.name: vault_file_created
assertions.description: A .md file was written to the Obsidian vault at /Users/bianders/morphy/ (with_skill only)

assertions.name: filename_title_case
assertions.description: Filename is 'Pydantic Validators.md' — title case with space

assertions.name: context_anchored
assertions.description: Content references FastAPI or the @validator vs @field_validator distinction — shows it used the conversation context
